competency_question: Can a drug-response framework that represents both inhibitory and stimulatory signaling, and that uses potency and efficacy in a combined index along with a cohort-level differential index, provide a more accurate basis for interpreting compound activity, comparing responses across diverse dose-response shapes, and identifying genotype-specific vulnerabilities or feedback-driven polarity shifts in high-throughput cancer screening—phenomena that traditional unidirectional metrics such as IC50, EC50, and AUC fail to capture?
protocol_adaptation: Literature-anchored synthesis (Fuzzy-to-Fact adapted for methodology CQ — no gene/drug CURIEs to resolve; entities are frameworks, metrics, and datasets)

properties.description: Per-division metrics for drug potency (GR50) and efficacy (GRmax) that correct for confounders from unequal division rates. Superior to IC50/Emax for dividing cells but does not explicitly model stimulatory responses or cohort-level differential scoring.

Integrates multiple dose-response curve features into a single score. The selective variant (sDSS) normalizes patient responses against healthy controls, functioning as a cohort-level differential index. Applied in leukemia precision medicine.

Uses both positive and negative controls to capture lethal, growth-inhibitory, AND growth-stimulatory drug behaviors from a single viability readout. The only identified metric that explicitly differentiates stimulatory from inhibitory modes — directly addresses the CQ's bidirectional requirement.

Decouples synergistic potency (alpha) from synergistic efficacy (beta) via 2D Hill equation. Handles bidirectional scenarios where a combination can be synergistic in potency but antagonistic in efficacy. Primarily designed for drug combinations rather than single-agent screening.

Characterizes biphasic U-shaped or inverted-U dose-response relationships where low doses stimulate and high doses inhibit. The Delta_X parameter (IC50 minus toxic threshold) is inversely related to stimulatory magnitude. Validated in NCI yeast anticancer drug screen. Directly models the polarity shift phenomenon central to the CQ.

limitations: Cannot be calculated when 50% inhibition not achieved
Does not distinguish cytostatic from cytotoxic effects
Misrepresents drug potency when growth rates vary
Captures both potency and efficacy but cannot disentangle them

properties.context: Used by Calabrese to validate hormetic dose-response model
properties.relevance: Demonstrated biphasic dose-response in anticancer compounds

nodes.label: Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC)

properties.context: Large-scale cancer cell line drug screening
properties.relevance: Standard dataset where IC50/AUC limitations are most visible
Paired genomic/drug response data for genotype-vulnerability analysis

nodes.label: HMS LINCS Breast Cancer Drug Response

properties.context: ~4,700 breast cancer cell line + perturbagen pairs
properties.relevance: Primary dataset for GR metrics validation; showed potency and efficacy are only weakly correlated

properties.description: Drug response switches from inhibitory to stimulatory (or vice versa) due to compensatory feedback signaling
examples: Paradoxical activation in BRAF-mutant melanoma with RAF inhibitors

properties.description: Drug sensitivity patterns that differ by cancer genotype (e.g., KRAS-mutant vs wild-type responses)

properties.improvement: Per-division potency is more biologically meaningful
Integrates curve shape features into single composite score; sDSS adds cohort differential

properties.mechanism: Positive and negative controls enable detection of growth stimulation
Delta_X parameter quantifies relationship between potency and stimulatory magnitude
Independent alpha parameters allow opposing potency synergies
sDSS normalizes patient vs. healthy controls
Removing division-rate confounders reveals true genotype-drug associations

properties.reason: Assumes monotonic inhibition; cannot represent growth above baseline
Integrates area under curve without sign awareness; stimulation cancels inhibition
Division rate differences between genotypes create artefactual sensitivity correlations

cq_answer_synthesis.verdict: PARTIALLY_SUPPORTED — No single existing framework fully matches all criteria in the CQ, but the evidence strongly supports the premise

bidirectional_inhibitory_stimulatory.key_finding: NDR (Gupta et al., 2020) explicitly differentiates lethal, growth-inhibitory, and growth-stimulatory modes. Hormesis models (Calabrese) established the quantitative relationship between potency and biphasic stimulatory magnitude.

combined_potency_efficacy_index.key_finding: GR metrics provide per-division potency (GR50) + efficacy (GRmax). MuSyC decouples alpha (potency) and beta (efficacy) in combination screens. DSS integrates multiple curve features into a composite score.

cohort_level_differential_index.key_finding: The selective DSS (sDSS) normalizes patient drug responses against healthy control cells, directly implementing a cohort-level differential index for identifying cancer-selective drugs.

polarity_shift_detection.key_finding: NDR detects growth stimulation from single viability readouts. Hormesis model's Delta_X parameter quantifies the inverse relationship between toxic potency and stimulatory magnitude.

genotype_specific_vulnerabilities.key_finding: GR metrics remove division-rate confounders that create artefactual genotype-sensitivity correlations (Hafner 2016). sDSS identifies genotype-selective drug effects via patient-vs-control normalization.

unified_bidirectional_framework.key_finding: No single published framework simultaneously satisfies ALL CQ criteria: (1) bidirectional response modeling, (2) combined potency-efficacy index, (3) cohort-level differential index, (4) polarity shift detection, and (5) genotype-specific vulnerability identification. The CQ describes a COMPOSITE framework that would need to synthesize elements from NDR (bidirectional), GR metrics (potency-efficacy decoupling), DSS/sDSS (differential scoring), and hormesis models (polarity shift quantification).

validation_verdicts.no_unified_framework_exists: UNVERIFIABLE — Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence; niche/recent work may not yet be indexed
